title: Crude Membrane Protein Extraction from Tissues
author: Vivian Liu
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.djt4nm

steps.title: Thaw Tissue/Cells and Prepare High Salt Buffer
steps.instruction: Thaw 20–50 mg of tissue or 15CM dish cells in 1 mL of high salt buffer. High salt buffer: To impair of ionic bonds.2 M NaCl, 10 mM HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.4, and 1 mM EDTA. Store at 4°C.

steps.title: Homogenize Tissues
steps.instruction: Homogenize tissues using an IKA Ultra Turbax blender at maximum speed ( ∼25,000rpm) for 30 s.

steps.title: Ultracentrifuge Suspension
steps.instruction: Ultracentrifuge the suspension in Beckman MLA 130 at 100,000 g for 10 min. The tubes should be balanced to a difference less than 50 mg.

steps.title: Discard Supernatant and Homogenize Pellet
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and homogenize pellets in 1 mL of carbonate buffer as in step 2 . Carbonate buffer: To allow efficient removal of soluble proteins1 M Na2CO3 and 1 mM EDTA, pH 11.3. Store at 4°C.

steps.title: Incubate with Mixing
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 min with gentle mixing.

steps.title: Collect Non-soluble Material by Centrifugation
steps.instruction: Collect the non-soluble material by centrifugation as in step 3 (If the content of integral membrane proteins in the purified membranes is not at least 30–40% of total identified proteins, steps 4–6 can be repeated two to three times).

steps.title: Resuspend Pellet in Wash Buffer
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant and resuspend pellets in wash buffer as in step 2 . Wash buffer: To melt non-integral membrane proteins.4 M urea, 100 mM NaCl, 10 mM HEPES-NaOH, pH 7.4, and 1 mM EDTA.

steps.title: Collect Crude Membranes by Centrifugation
steps.instruction: Collect the crude membranes by centrifugation as in step 3 .
